title: daviesia corymbosa var. arborea
intro: Daviesia corymbosa var. arborea, also known as the Mountain Pea or Bush Pea, is a shrub native to eastern Australia, belonging to the Fabaceae family. The plant is traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of Daviesia corymbosa var. arborea include relief from pain, inflammation, and skin conditions, as well as its potential to support immune function and reduce fever. Herbal preparations of the plant are made from its leaves and stems, which can be used in teas, infusions, or as a tincture to harness its medicinal properties.
